ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS MODIFIED - PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO ITS REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS IS DENIED. THE INCLUSION OF "REFERRED TO" IN THE REQUEST IS CONFUSING. MOREOVER, PLAINTIFF HAS NOT SUFFICIENTLY MET AND CONFERRED WITH DEFENDANT ON MANY OF THE REQUESTS. FINALLY, DEFENDANT HAS PROVIDED MANY OF THE DOCUMENTS. EXCEPT AS TO CERTAIN RFP'S, THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: RFP #2 DEFENDANT TO PROVIDE REDACTED BILLING STATEMENTS WITH PRIVILEGE LOG IF NECESSARY; RFP #3 DEFENDANT MUST PROVIDE A TENDER LETTER; RFP #4 DEFENDANT MUST PROVIDE THE REJECTION LETTER; RFP #16 DEFENDANT MUST PROVIDE THEIR INSURANCE POLICY; RFP #23 DEFENDANT MUST PROVIDE A COPY OF THE SETTLEMENT CHECK; RFP #69, RFP #71 & RFP #72 - PLAINTIFF WILL DEPOSE DEFENDANT'S CEO ON THE ISSUE RELEVANT TO THESE REQUESTS. DEFENDANT MUST THEN PROVIDE ANY DOCUMENTS WITNESS RELIES UPON IN RESPONDING TO QUESTIONS ON THIS ISSUE. DEFENDANT TO PREPARE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI; NOT REPORTED

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF INTERSTATE FIRE AND CASUALTY INSURANCE COMPANY'S AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT TOOK THE MATTER UNDER SUBMISSION. COURT RULED ON DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE, OVERRULED IN PART & SUSTAINED IN PART. ORDER ON OBJECTIONS SIGNED. --- AFTER HEARING THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED - PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED. UNDER LINDEN PARTNERS V. WILSHIRE LINDEN ASSOC., (1998) 62 CAL.APP.4TH 508, 522, THE COURT CAN SUMMARY ADJUDICATE WHETHER OR NOT DEFENDANT OWED A DUTY TO DEFEND. THE LANGUAGE OF THE SUBCONTRACT AND THE FRISBY AMENDED COMPLAINT (CONSIDERED BY THIS COURT NOT FOR THE TRUTH OF ITS ALLEGATIONS BUT TO SHOW WHETHER ALLEGATIONS TRIGGERED COVERAGE), PLAINTIFF HAS SUFFICIENTLY EVIDENCED THAT THE INDEMNITY PROVISION WAS TRIGGERED AND THE DEFENDANT OWED A DUTY TO DEFEND PLAINTIFF. ACCORDINGLY, THE BURDEN SHIFTED TO DEFENDANT TO SHOW THAT THE HARM TO FRISBY WAS SOLELY CAUSED BY WEBCOR. IT FAILED TO DO SO. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR A CONTINUANCE TO CONDUCT FURTHER DISCOVERY IS DENIED. DEFENDANT HAS FAILED TO MAKE A SUFFICIENT SHOWING AS TO WHAT IT MAY SPECIFICALLY UNDERCOVER THAT WOULD RAISE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. PLAINTIFF'S ORDER ON MOTION SIGNED (WITH ADDITIONAL LANGUAGE). JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CLEVELAND WRECKING COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FOR SUBROGATION. RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT IN THE FRISBY CASE CUT OFF WEBCOR'S ABILITY TO SUE CLEVELAND FOR INDEMNITY OR CONTRIBUTION FOR ITS ALLEGED NEGLIGENT CONDUCT. AT THE SAME TIME, WEBCOR HAS NO CLAIM AGAINST CLEVELAND FOR CLEVELAND'S BREACH OF ITS DUTY TO DEFEND WEBCOR BECAUSE WEBCOR HAS SUSTAINED NO DAMAGES AS A CONSEQUENCE OF THE BREACH. IT IS FOR THIS REASON THAT INTERSTATE'S EQUITABLE POSITION IS NOT SUPERIOR TO CLEVELAND'S EQUITABLE POSITION. BRAMALEA CALIF., INC. V. RELIABLE INTERIORS, INC. (2004) 119 CAL. APP. 4TH 468; PATENT SCAFFOLDING CO. V. WILLIAM SIMPSON CONST. CO. (1967) 256 CAL. APP. 2D 506. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

BRASCH'S TESTIMONY INDICATES THAT HIS DEALINGS WITH THE FRISKY LITIGATION WERE SOMEWHAT LIMITED AND TO THE EXTENT THAT HE PARTICIPATED IN THE TENDER MATTER AND DOES NOT RECALL SPECIFIC CONVERSATIONS, AN ORDER TO COMPEL WILL NOT REFRESH HIS RECOLLECTION. FURTHER, CLEVELAND CLAIMS TO HAVE A RECORD OF THE CONVERSATION AND WHAT WAS DISCUSSED. WEBCOR HAS ALSO AGREED TO PROVIDE PRIOR DEPOSITION TESTIMONY OF ITS EMPLOYEES INVOLVED IN THE FRISKY ACTION TO ASSIST CLEVELAND IN ITS QUEST TO DETERMINE LIABILITY FOR THE UNDERLYING INCIDENT. AS TO THE QUESTION TO MR. BRASCH REGARDING INTERPRETATION OF THE SUB-CONTRACT, THERE IS NO CLEAR NOTICE THAT THIS WOULD BE A MATTER FOR EXAMINATION AND IT IS HIGHLY QUESTIONABLE THAT HIS INTERPRETATION IS EVEN RELEVANT. RULING: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling - DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEPOSITION TESTIMONY OF MR. BRASCH IS DENIED. FIRST, MR. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar May-04-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
PLAINTIFF INTERSTATE FIRE AND CASUALTY INSURANCE COMPANY'S AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.

APPEARANCES: David W. Gordon, Esq. of Kenney & Markowitz L.L.P. 397-3100 for pltf & moving party; Giovanna A. Ferrari, Esq. of Seyfarth Shaw LLP 397-2823 for deft & opposing party.

RULING: Argued and the Court took the matter under submission.
Court ruled on defendant's objections to evidence, overruled in part & sustained in part. Order on objections signed.
After hearing the Court ruled as follows: Tentative ruling adopted - PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED. UNDER LINDEN PARTNERS V. WILSHIRE LINDEN ASSOC., (1998) 62 CAL.APP.4TH 508, 522, THE COURT CAN SUMMARY ADJUDICATE WHETHER OR NOT DEFENDANT OWED A DUTY TO DEFEND. THE LANGUAGE OF THE SUBCONTRACT AND THE FRISBY AMENDED COMPLAINT (CONSIDERED BY THIS COURT NOT FOR THE TRUTH OF ITS ALLEGATIONS BUT TO SHOW WHETHER ALLEGATIONS TRIGGERED COVERAGE), PLAINTIFF HAS SUFFICIENTLY EVIDENCED THAT THE INDEMNITY PROVISION WAS TRIGGERED AND THE DEFENDANT OWED A DUTY TO DEFEND PLAINTIFF. ACCORDINGLY, THE BURDEN SHIFTED TO DEFENDANT TO SHOW THAT THE HARM TO FRISBY WAS SOLELY CAUSED BY WEBCOR. IT FAILED TO DO SO. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR A CONTINUANCE TO CONDUCT FURTHER DISCOVERY IS DENIED. DEFENDANT HAS FAILED TO MAKE A SUFFICIENT SHOWING AS TO WHAT IT MAY SPECIFICALLY UNDERCOVER THAT WOULD RAISE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT.
Plaintiff's order on motion signed (with additional language).
(302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-25-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CLEVELAND WRECKING COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FOR SUBROGATION.
APPEARANCES: David W. Gordon, Esq. of Kenney & Markowitz L.L.P. 397-3100 for pltf & opposing party; Jonathan A. Braunstein, Esq. of Seyfarth Shaw LLP 397-2823 for deft & moving party.
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - sustained without leave to amend. The good faith settlement in the Frisby case cut off Webcor's ability to sue Cleveland for indemnity or contribution for its alleged negligent conduct. At the same time, Webcor has no claim against Cleveland for Cleveland's breach of its duty to defend Webcor because Webcor has sustained no damages as a consequence of the breach. It is for this reason that Interstate's equitable position is not superior to Cleveland's equitable position. Bramalea Calif., Inc. v. Reliable Interiors, Inc. (2004) 119 Cal. App. 4th 468; Patent Scaffolding Co. v. William Simpson Const. Co. (1967) 256 Cal. App. 2d 506. (302/PJM/JW) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-08-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jonathan Braunstein of Seyfarth Shaw (397.2823) for defendant and moving party
David Gordon of Kenney & Markowitz (397.3100) for plaintiff and responding party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT CLEVAND WRECKING COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained with 10 days leave to amend. Plaintiff in its opposition asserts that defendant engaged in tortious conduct that gave rise to the loss. That is not plead. On its face, the complaint fails to state a claim for equitable subrogation. The decision in Patent Scaffolding Co. v. Wm. Simpson Constr. Co. (1967) 256 Cal App 2d 506 and Bramalea Calif., Inc. v. Reliable Interiors, Inc. (2004) 119 Cal App 4th 468 are authority to sustain the demurrer for failure to state a claim. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/PJM/VC) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600
